response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial passion (founded an open-source Geo-Data platform used by Green Map, built web GIS apps) and real low-latency systems experience (DLang services handling millions of requests per second with async I/O, fibers, and caching). However, their modern C++ exposure is dated — limited to an early-career role on LabWindows/CVI and a bachelor thesis — and their recent work centers on TypeScript/Node full-stack and AI tooling rather than routing pipelines or navigation systems. DLang expertise is a reasonable proxy for C++/Rust systems thinking, but the gap in production C++/Rust and routing-tile-specific work makes this a stretch fit worth a screening conversation rather than a clear yes.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain experience, including founding and operating an open-source mapping platform
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching, fibers)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and DevOps fluency
concerns: No recent commercial C++ or any Rust experience; systems work is primarily in DLang
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience — GIS background is web-mapping oriented
Recent roles skew toward full-stack web and AI tooling, away from the data-pipeline/infrastructure focus of this role

key_factors.factor: Lack of modern C++/Rust production experience
Geospatial domain familiarity via GIS projects
